name: CANCER_HIGH_EXPRESSION_FOR_TOP_ASSIGNEE_TARGETPAIRS
description: Cancer high expression for top assignee target pairs. Auto-generated from the core-four benchmark and aligned to the validated query-frame Cypher semantics.
cypher:
MATCH (ymax:Year)
WITH max(ymax.year) AS max_year
MATCH (tp:TargetPair)<-[:HAS_TARGET_PAIR]-(p0:Patent)-[:PUBLISHED_IN]->(y0:Year)
WITH tp, min(y0.year) AS first_year, max_year
WHERE first_year >= max_year - $years + 1
MATCH (tp)<-[:HAS_TARGET_PAIR]-(p:Patent)-[:HAS_ASSIGNEE]->(a:Assignee)
WITH a, tp, count(DISTINCT p) AS patent_count
ORDER BY patent_count DESC
WITH a, collect(DISTINCT tp)[0..10] AS tps
UNWIND tps AS tp
MATCH (tp)-[:HAS_TARGET]->(t:Target)-[:DIFFERENTIAL_AND_HIGHLY_EXPRESSED_IN]->(c:Cancer)
RETURN a.name AS assignee, tp.name AS target_pair, collect(DISTINCT c.name) AS high_expression_cancers, collect(DISTINCT t.symbol) AS targets
ORDER BY assignee, target_pair
LIMIT 100

examples: Among the novel combinations associated with those highly active companies, which involve targets that are highly expressed in cancer, and in which cancer types?
For those highly active companies' novel combinations, which target pairs contain cancer-highly-expressed targets, and what cancers are implicated?
Which of the novel combinations tied to those active companies involve targets highly expressed in tumors, and in which tumor types are they highly expressed?
